# onboarding

> Minimal MCP server for Rocket.Chat generated by rc-mcp.

**Generated by [rc-mcp](https://github.com/thekishandev/MCP-Server-Generator)** — Minimal MCP Server Generator for Rocket.Chat.

## Tools (4)

| Tool | Method | Endpoint | Description |
|------|--------|----------|-------------|
| `onboard_user` | GET | `/api/v1/users.info` | Look up a user by username, resolve a room by name, invite them to the room, and send a welcome message. Spans user-management + rooms + ... |
| `send_dm_to_user` | POST | `/api/v1/dm.create` | Open a direct message conversation with a user and send them a message. |
| `groups_invite` | POST | `/api/v1/groups.invite` | Invite Users to Group. POST groups.invite API wrapper. Requires: roomId, userId. |
| `login` | POST | `/api/v1/login` | Login with Username and Password |

## Authentication

This server uses **auto-authentication from environment variables**. The `.env` file is pre-configured with your credentials — individual tools do NOT require `authToken` or `userId` params. Auth is handled automatically at startup.

Required env vars:
- **RC_URL** — your Rocket.Chat server URL
- **RC_AUTH_TOKEN** — your X-Auth-Token
- **RC_USER_ID** — your X-User-Id

## Quick Start

### 1. Install dependencies

\`\`\`bash
npm install
\`\`\`

### 2. Configure environment

\`\`\`bash
cp .env.example .env
# Only RC_URL is needed (defaults to http://localhost:3000)
\`\`\`

### 3. Build and run

\`\`\`bash
npm run build
npm start
\`\`\`

### 4. Use with gemini-cli

Add to your \`settings.json\`:

\`\`\`json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "onboarding": {
      "command": "node",
      "args": ["./dist/server.js"],
      "env": {
        "RC_URL": "http://localhost:3000"
      }
    }
  }
}
\`\`\`

Then just tell gemini-cli what you want to do:
\`\`\`
> send a message to channel #general
\`\`\`
The agent will ask for your Auth Token, User ID, and other required params.

## Development

\`\`\`bash
# Run in development mode (no build step)
npm run dev

# Run tests
npm test
\`\`\`
